title: Time Traveler
subtitle: Reconstruct the correct config from 5 historical versions

briefing.narrative:
INCIDENT REPORT: config.txt has been modified 5 times across 4 tagged releases. Each version changed different settings. The current version is a disaster — wrong password, wrong port, and a critical feature flag that was accidentally removed.

The Head Monk slides you a timeline:
- v1.0: Initial config with the correct feature flag (enable_backups: true)
- v2.0: Changed to the correct database password (db_pass: monastery_secure_789)
- v3.0: Set the correct port (port: 8443)
- v4.0: Someone broke the password AND removed the backup flag
- Current: Someone also broke the port

"Three problems in the current config. Each correct value lives in a DIFFERENT historical version. You must use git show to read each tagged version, find the correct values, and assemble the one true config."

The cat has memorized every version of every config file. But it's not going to help.

"This is temporal archaeology. Dig through the layers. Find the truth."
briefing.concept:
Git stores the complete content of every file at every commit. You can read any historical version:

git tag — list all tags
git show v1.0:config.txt — read config.txt as it was at tag v1.0
git log --oneline — see the commit history

When the current version has multiple problems, each fix may come from a different point in history. You need to investigate each version to find which one has the correct value for each setting.
objectives: Use git log and git tag to understand the history
Use git show to read config.txt at each tagged version
Fix the 3 problems: restore password from v2.0, port from v3.0, and backup flag from v1.0
The final config must have ALL correct values combined

args.content:
database: postgres
host: prod.monastery.io
port: 5432
db_pass: initial_pass
max_connections: 100
enable_backups: true
log_level: info

database: postgres
host: prod.monastery.io
port: 5432
db_pass: monastery_secure_789
max_connections: 100
enable_backups: true
log_level: info

database: postgres
host: prod.monastery.io
port: 8443
db_pass: monastery_secure_789
max_connections: 200
enable_backups: true
log_level: warn

database: postgres
host: prod.monastery.io
port: 8443
db_pass: LEAKED_password
max_connections: 200
log_level: warn

database: postgres
host: prod.monastery.io
port: 3000
db_pass: LEAKED_password
max_connections: 200
log_level: debug

hints.text: Use git log --oneline to see the history. Then git show HEAD~N:config.txt for each version to find which had the correct password, port, and backup flag.
The password 'monastery_secure_789' is from HEAD~3. The port '8443' is from HEAD~2. The 'enable_backups: true' line is from HEAD~4. Combine them all into the current config.
